title: New Treatment for Type 1 Diabetes Now Available on the NHS
subtitle: A breakthrough drug offers patients years without the daily challenges of managing the condition.

summary.en: A new immunotherapy drug called teplizumab has been approved for use in England and Wales. It is the first treatment capable of delaying the onset of type 1 diabetes by up to three years. The drug works by training the immune system to stop attacking the pancreas. Experts describe the approval as a historic shift in how the disease is managed. While the drug is a major step forward, advocates continue to push for better screening programs.
summary.zh: 一種名為 teplizumab 的新型免疫療法藥物已獲准在英格蘭與威爾斯使用。這是首款能將第一型糖尿病發作時間延後長達三年的治療藥物。該藥物透過訓練免疫系統停止攻擊胰臟來發揮作用。專家形容此核准是疾病管理方式的歷史性轉變。儘管該藥物是一大進步，倡議人士仍持續推動建立更完善的篩檢計畫。

paragraphs.en: A significant medical breakthrough is now reaching patients in England and Wales. Following recent approval, the National Health Service (NHS) is making a new drug called teplizumab available to those at risk of developing type 1 diabetes. This development represents the first time in over a century that a treatment has been able to change the course of the disease rather than just managing its symptoms.
paragraphs.zh: 一項重大的醫學突破現正造福英格蘭與威爾斯的病患。在近期獲得核准後，英國國家醫療保健服務（NHS）已開始為第一型糖尿病高風險族群提供一種名為「teplizumab」的新藥。這項發展是超過一個世紀以來，首次出現能改變疾病病程，而不僅僅是控制症狀的治療方式。

paragraphs.en: Type 1 diabetes is a condition where the body’s immune system mistakenly attacks the cells in the pancreas that produce insulin. Insulin is a vital hormone that helps the body turn sugar into energy. For decades, the only way to treat the disease has been to replace the missing insulin through daily injections or a pump. This requires constant monitoring of blood sugar levels, which creates a heavy, lifelong burden for patients and their families. According to The Guardian, this new immunotherapy drug does not cure the condition, but it can delay the start of symptoms for up to three years.
paragraphs.zh: 第一型糖尿病是一種身體免疫系統誤攻擊胰臟中胰島素分泌細胞的疾病。胰島素是幫助身體將糖分轉化為能量的關鍵荷爾蒙。數十年來，治療該疾病的唯一方法是透過每日注射或使用幫浦來補充缺失的胰島素。這需要持續監測血糖濃度，對病患及其家屬造成沉重且終身的負擔。根據《衛報》報導，這種新型免疫療法藥物雖無法治癒疾病，但能將症狀發作的時間延後長達三年。

paragraphs.en: This delay offers a meaningful period of time for children and adults to live without the daily pressures of the disease. For many families, these extra years are highly valuable. Karen Addington, chief executive of the charity Breakthrough T1D, noted that if it were a loved one, anyone would want to provide them with more time free from the relentless demands of managing the condition. The NHS medicines body described the decision to approve the drug as genuinely exciting, with hundreds of young people expected to benefit each year.
paragraphs.zh: 這段延遲期為兒童與成人提供了寶貴的時間，讓他們能暫時擺脫疾病帶來的日常壓力。對許多家庭而言，這些額外的時間極具價值。慈善機構「Breakthrough T1D」執行長凱倫·艾丁頓（Karen Addington）指出，若是自己的摯愛，任何人都不希望他們過早承受管理病情所帶來的無情要求。英國國家醫療保健服務的藥物審核機構形容這項核准決定令人振奮，預計每年將有數百名年輕病患受惠。

paragraphs.en: Teplizumab is designed for children and adults aged eight and older who are in the early stages of the disease, known as stage 2. The treatment process involves receiving the medicine through a drip into a vein once a day for 14 days. By training the immune system to stop attacking the pancreas, the drug provides a window of relief before the disease progresses to the point where full-time insulin therapy is required. Dr. Elizabeth Robertson of Diabetes UK stated that this marks a new age of treatment, moving beyond simple insulin replacement to target the root cause of the condition.
paragraphs.zh: Teplizumab 適用於八歲以上、處於疾病早期階段（即第二期）的兒童與成人。治療過程需連續十四天，每日透過靜脈點滴注射藥物。該藥物透過訓練免疫系統停止攻擊胰臟，在疾病惡化至需要全天候胰島素治療前，提供了一段緩衝期。英國糖尿病協會（Diabetes UK）的伊莉莎白·羅伯森（Elizabeth Robertson）博士表示，這標誌著治療的新紀元，從單純的胰島素補充跨越到針對疾病根源進行治療。

paragraphs.en: Despite the excitement surrounding the drug, experts point out that there are still challenges to overcome. The primary issue is detection. Because the drug must be given before symptoms appear, patients need to know they are at risk early. Currently, there is no routine screening program for type 1 diabetes in the UK. Most people only find out they are at risk through blood tests done for other medical reasons or because they have a family history of the condition. However, BBC Health reports that most people diagnosed with the disease have no close family history, which suggests that environmental factors may also play a role.
paragraphs.zh: 儘管各界對此藥物感到興奮，專家仍指出尚有挑戰待克服。首要問題在於檢測。由於藥物必須在症狀出現前使用，病患需儘早得知自己處於高風險狀態。目前，英國並無針對第一型糖尿病的常規篩檢計畫。大多數人僅是在因其他醫療原因進行血液檢查時，或是因為有家族病史才發現自己處於風險中。然而，BBC 健康頻道報導指出，大多數確診病患並無近親家族病史，這顯示環境因素可能也扮演了一定角色。

paragraphs.en: Italy is currently the only country in the world with a national screening program for children. In the UK, diabetes charities are actively campaigning for similar testing to be introduced. Without a way to identify those at risk, many who could benefit from the drug might not be diagnosed in time to receive it. For now, the focus remains on ensuring that those who are eligible can access the treatment fairly.
paragraphs.zh: 義大利目前是世界上唯一擁有兒童全國篩檢計畫的國家。在英國，糖尿病慈善團體正積極爭取推動類似的檢測機制。若缺乏識別高風險族群的方法，許多本可受惠於此藥物的病患，可能無法及時獲得診斷以接受治療。目前，重點仍放在確保符合資格的病患能公平地取得治療。

paragraphs.en: The approval of teplizumab is the result of decades of research. While it is not a permanent solution, it provides a vital bridge for patients. For parents like Vicky and Ben, whose son Theo was diagnosed at a young age, the possibility of delaying the onset of the disease is life-changing. They noted that having those extra years of childhood without the constant worry of blood sugar levels and insulin calculations would make a massive difference to families across the country. As medical professionals look toward the future, many hope this is the first step toward eventually preventing type 1 diabetes altogether.
paragraphs.zh: Teplizumab 的核准是數十年研究的成果。雖然它並非永久性的解決方案，但為病患提供了一座關鍵的橋樑。對於像薇琪（Vicky）與班（Ben）這樣兒子在年幼時就確診的父母來說，延緩疾病發作的可能性足以改變人生。他們表示，若能擁有這些額外的童年時光，不必時刻擔憂血糖數值與胰島素計算，對全國各地的家庭而言將有巨大的影響。隨著醫療專業人員展望未來，許多人期盼這能成為最終徹底預防第一型糖尿病的第一步。

keyTerms.definitionEn: The natural development or progress of something over time.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作「課程」，這裡指疾病發展的進程。文中：This development represents the first time in over a century that a treatment has been able to change the course of the disease rather than just managing its symptoms.

keyTerms.definitionEn: A limited period of time during which an opportunity exists.
keyTerms.definitionZh: （機會的）空檔、短暫的時間窗口。
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作「窗戶」，這裡指一段特定的時間機會。文中：By training the immune system to stop attacking the pancreas, the drug provides a window of relief before the disease progresses to the point where full-time insulin therapy is required.

keyTerms.definitionEn: Something that provides a connection or transition between two things.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 橋樑（比喻過渡階段或連結）。
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作實體建築的「橋」，這裡指治療上的過渡手段。文中：While it is not a permanent solution, it provides a vital bridge for patients.

sources.name: BBC Health
sources.title: First drug to delay onset of type 1 diabetes made available on NHS

sources.name: The Guardian Health
sources.title: Drug that delays onset of type 1 diabetes approved in England and Wales

previousArticles.title: New Drug Offers Hope by Delaying Type 1 Diabetes
previousArticles.summaryEn: A new drug called teplizumab has been approved for use in England and Wales. It is the first treatment that can delay the start of type 1 diabetes for up to three years. The medicine works by training the immune system to stop attacking the body's own cells. Experts describe this as a major step forward in managing the condition. However, patients must be identified early to receive the treatment.

quiz.question: What is the specific procedure required to administer the new drug, teplizumab?

options.A: A daily insulin injection performed by the patient at home.
options.B: A 14-day course of treatment delivered intravenously.
options.C: A monthly infusion session at a local hospital for children.
options.D: A one-time medical procedure to target the pancreas directly.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案是 B。根據文章第四段，治療過程是透過靜脈點滴（drip into a vein）每天給藥一次，持續 14 天。選項 A 錯誤，因為這是傳統胰島素治療的方式而非新藥。選項 C 錯誤，因為文中並未提到每月一次。選項 D 錯誤，因為這不是一次性的程序，且其目的是調整免疫系統而非直接針對胰臟進行手術。

quiz.question: Why might many people who are currently at risk for type 1 diabetes be unable to access teplizumab?

options.A: The drug is only available to patients who have a documented family history of the condition.
options.B: The NHS has limited the number of patients allowed to receive the treatment each year.
options.C: There is no systematic way to identify asymptomatic individuals who qualify for the drug.
options.D: The drug is too expensive for the National Health Service to provide to the general public.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案是 C。根據第五段與第六段，該藥物必須在症狀出現前（stage 2）使用，但英國目前缺乏常規篩檢計畫。因此，許多潛在患者無法在關鍵時刻被診斷出來，導致無法及時獲得治療。選項 A 錯誤，因為文章提到大多數患者並無家族史。選項 B 錯誤，文中未提及數量限制。選項 D 錯誤，文中並未將成本列為無法使用的主要原因。

quiz.question: In the final paragraph, what does the author mean by calling the drug a 'vital bridge'?

options.A: It connects the patient to a permanent cure for the disease.
options.B: It provides a temporary period of relief before the disease progresses.
options.C: It is a physical medical device that helps transport insulin.
options.D: It acts as a communication tool between doctors and patients.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案是 B。在文中，作者使用「bridge」（橋樑）一詞是隱喻，指該藥物雖然無法根治疾病，但能提供一段寶貴的緩衝時間（delay the onset），讓患者免於承受嚴重症狀，直到未來可能有更進一步的治療。選項 A 錯誤，因為文章明確提到這不是永久解決方案。選項 C 與 D 則是對該詞的字面誤解，完全偏離了醫學隱喻的意涵。

quiz.question: What is the primary message of this article regarding the treatment of type 1 diabetes?

options.A: The NHS has finally found a permanent cure for type 1 diabetes after decades of research.
options.B: Daily insulin injections are no longer necessary for children diagnosed with diabetes.
options.C: A new drug offers hope by delaying the disease, though early detection remains a significant hurdle.
options.D: Italy’s national screening program is the only model that the UK should follow to solve the diabetes crisis.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案是 C。全文的核心在於介紹 teplizumab 這項新突破，強調它能延緩病程（delay the onset），但也同時指出由於缺乏篩檢機制，目前在實際應用上仍面臨挑戰。選項 A 錯誤，文中多次強調這不是根治。選項 B 錯誤，文章未說胰島素注射不再需要。選項 D 錯誤，義大利的例子僅作為對比，並非文章的核心主旨。
